metric_value: 'The next form factor battles are beginning to be fought in the living room' — Peter Kastner, Aberdeen Group, via Reuters
notes: Widely-propagated 2004 Kastner framing of the living-room-PC opportunity

metric_name: Kastner on size and decor mattering
metric_value: 'Factors including size and decor suddenly matter a lot' — Kastner noting PC buyers in 2004 started caring about form factor

metric_name: Seward skepticism on PC in living room
metric_value: 'Why put a PC where it has to compete with other things for your attention? ... adding a computer only makes the relatively simple process of watching TV far more complicated' — Seward
Consumers pay $50 more for iPod mini (1/3 the capacity of larger iPod) citing color ('It's pinker') — demonstrates style-primary purchasing in consumer electronics
Reuters/Kastner article predicted PCs would be designed to sit in the living room with less bulk and more style

metric_name: Streaming boxes win living room, not PCs
metric_value: By 2015 the living-room-content-delivery battle was won by streaming appliances (Apple TV 2007, Roku 2008, Chromecast 2013, Fire TV 2014) — not general-purpose PCs. Windows Media Center was discontinued in Windows 10 (2015). Kastner's broader 'form factor battles in living room' was correct, but the form factor that won was the TV-attached appliance, not the living-room PC.
notes: Partial vindication of Kastner; full vindication of Seward
